name: Marbury v. Madison
holding: It is emphatically the province of the judicial department to say what the law is; statute conflicting with the Constitution is void.
facts_one_liner: After-midnight appointee never received his commission as Justice of the Peace and sought mandamus.

name: Correspondence of the Justices
holding: Federal courts may not issue advisory opinions to the political branches.
facts_one_liner: Washington and Jefferson asked Justices for legal opinions on neutrality and treaties amid French hostilities.

name: Hayburn's Case
holding: Federal judges may not perform non-judicial functions subject to executive revision.
facts_one_liner: Pension claims statute required circuit judges to make recommendations subject to Secretary of War review.

name: Frothingham v. Mellon
holding: Taxpayer's interest in federal expenditure is too remote and indeterminate to support standing.
facts_one_liner: Massachusetts taxpayer challenged Maternity Act's federal grants to states.

name: Flast v. Cohen
holding: Taxpayers have standing to challenge spending under the Establishment Clause if a nexus exists between status and constitutional limit.
facts_one_liner: Taxpayers challenged ESEA funds going to religious schools.

name: Allen v. Wright
holding: Injury must be fairly traceable to defendant's conduct; chain through third-party private school choices breaks traceability.
facts_one_liner: Parents alleged IRS failed to deny tax exemptions to racially discriminatory private schools.

name: Lujan v. Defenders of Wildlife
holding: Standing requires concrete and imminent injury; Congress cannot manufacture standing without injury.
facts_one_liner: Environmentalists challenged Interior's restriction of Endangered Species Act consultation to U.S. activities.

name: Clapper v. Amnesty International
holding: Injury must be certainly impending; chain of speculation about government surveillance insufficient.
facts_one_liner: Lawyers challenged FISA Amendments Act before any surveillance.

name: California v. Texas
holding: No standing to challenge a statute imposing no penalty.
facts_one_liner: States and individuals challenged ACA individual mandate after Congress zeroed the penalty.

name: TransUnion v. Ramirez
holding: Concrete harm requires close historical or common-law analog; only those whose reports were transmitted suffered defamation analog.
facts_one_liner: 1853 class members had wrong terrorist-list flags; only some had reports sent to third parties.

name: DeFunis v. Odegaard
holding: Mootness when no live controversy remains.
facts_one_liner: Law student near graduation had been admitted via lower court order.

name: Friends of the Earth v. Laidlaw
holding: Voluntary cessation moots only if absolutely clear conduct will not recur.
facts_one_liner: Polluter ceased violations during litigation but could resume.

name: City of Erie v. Pap's A.M.
holding: Case not moot when injunction's preclusive effects persist.
facts_one_liner: Nude dancing club closed during pendency; injunction still bound the city.

name: Abbott Laboratories v. Gardner
holding: Pre-enforcement challenge ripe when issue is purely legal and hardship from delay is significant.
facts_one_liner: Drug manufacturers challenged FDA labeling rule before enforcement.

name: United Public Workers v. Mitchell
holding: Generalized challenge unripe absent specific application.
facts_one_liner: Federal workers sought to enjoin Hatch Act before any violation.

name: Susan B. Anthony List v. Driehaus
holding: Pre-enforcement challenge ripe with credible threat of enforcement plus chilling speech.
facts_one_liner: Group challenged Ohio false-statement statute after past complaint.

name: Linda R.S. v. Richard D.
holding: No standing to compel criminal prosecution; injury not redressable by jailing the alleged father.
facts_one_liner: Mother sued for selective enforcement of nonsupport statute.

name: United States v. Richardson
holding: Taxpayer-citizen lacked standing to challenge CIA budget secrecy.
facts_one_liner: Plaintiff alleged CIA budget violated Statement and Account Clause.

name: Massachusetts v. EPA
holding: States get special solicitude in standing analysis.
facts_one_liner: States sued EPA for refusing to regulate GHG.

name: Murthy v. Missouri
holding: Standing must be traced to specific government conduct, not general atmosphere.
facts_one_liner: States challenged government communication with social media platforms.

name: Alliance for Hippocratic Medicine v. FDA
holding: Doctors lack standing where injury depends on speculative chain.
facts_one_liner: Doctors challenged FDA mifepristone approval.

name: Haaland v. Brackeen
holding: Relief must come from court's judgment, not from its opinion.
facts_one_liner: ICWA challenge where courts could not grant relief running against any defendant.

name: Summers v. Earth Island Institute
holding: Procedural injury alone insufficient without concrete underlying interest.
facts_one_liner: Environmental groups challenged Forest Service rule allowing logging.

name: Regents v. Bakke
holding: Frame injury as denial of equal opportunity to compete.
facts_one_liner: White applicant denied admission under racial quota.

name: Sheldon v. Sill
holding: Congress may withhold from inferior courts any case within Article III; assignee restriction valid.
facts_one_liner: Suit on assigned mortgage where original parties were diverse.

name: Ex parte McCardle
holding: Congress may strip Supreme Court appellate jurisdiction even mid-case.
facts_one_liner: Reconstruction-era newspaper editor's habeas appeal jurisdictionally stripped.

name: United States v. Klein
holding: Congress may not direct outcomes by manipulating jurisdiction.
facts_one_liner: Statute denied effect to presidential pardon as evidence of loyalty.

name: Ex parte Yerger
holding: McCardle didn't strip all habeas; alternate routes remain.
facts_one_liner: Habeas petitioner sought review under earlier statute after McCardle.

name: Boumediene v. Bush
holding: Suspension Clause limits Congress's power to strip habeas; Guantanamo detainees have constitutional right.
facts_one_liner: MCA stripped courts of jurisdiction over Guantanamo detainees.

name: Northern Pipeline v. Marathon
holding: Bankruptcy court may not adjudicate state-law claims; violates Article III.
facts_one_liner: Bankruptcy court heard state contract counterclaim.

name: Crowell v. Benson
holding: Agency may adjudicate public rights subject to Article III review on jurisdictional facts.
facts_one_liner: Compensation order under Longshoremen's Act.

name: Thomas v. Union Carbide
holding: Congress may channel federally created rights to non-Article III tribunals.
facts_one_liner: FIFRA arbitration of pesticide data compensation.

name: CFTC v. Schor
holding: Consent plus limited scope plus Article III review permits CFTC to hear common-law counterclaim.
facts_one_liner: Customer's reparations claim plus broker's state-law counterclaim before CFTC.

name: Stern v. Marshall
holding: Bankruptcy court cannot enter final judgment on state-law counterclaim not resolved by claim adjudication.
facts_one_liner: Vickie Lynn Marshall's tort counterclaim against son.

name: Wellness International v. Sharif
holding: Litigant consent allows bankruptcy judge to adjudicate Stern claim.
facts_one_liner: Debtor objected after years of consent.

name: United States v. Raddatz
holding: Magistrate may take suppression evidence subject to district court de novo review.
facts_one_liner: Magistrate held suppression hearing.

name: Peretz v. United States
holding: With consent, magistrate may conduct felony voir dire.
facts_one_liner: Defendant consented to magistrate-led voir dire.

name: Testa v. Katt
holding: State courts must enforce non-discriminatory federal law.
facts_one_liner: Rhode Island court refused to enforce price-control penalty.

name: Haywood v. Drown
holding: State may not bar § 1983 suits via seemingly neutral jurisdictional rule.
facts_one_liner: NY rerouted prisoner § 1983 claims to court of claims.

name: FERC v. Mississippi
holding: State courts may be required to enforce federal law; not anti-commandeering of executive.
facts_one_liner: PURPA required state utilities to consider federal standards.

name: Mondou v. New York N.H. & H.
holding: State court can't refuse FELA claims based on policy disagreement.
facts_one_liner: Connecticut court declined FELA jurisdiction.

name: McKnett v. St. Louis & SF
holding: State may not discriminate in jurisdiction between federal and state claims.
facts_one_liner: Alabama heard out-of-state state-law claims but rejected federal ones.

name: Douglas v. NY N.H. & H.
holding: Forum non conveniens-style dismissal may be valid excuse.
facts_one_liner: State dismissed FELA claim against in-state corporation by in-state plaintiff.

name: Dice v. Akron C.& Y.R.
holding: State procedural rule preempted where it defeats federal substantive policy.
facts_one_liner: Ohio applied state release-validity rule to FELA claim.

name: Minneapolis & St. L. v. Bombolis
holding: State may apply non-unanimous jury rule to federal claim.
facts_one_liner: FELA tried under Minnesota nonunanimous jury rule.

name: Felder v. Casey
holding: State notice-of-claim rule preempted in § 1983 actions.
facts_one_liner: Wisconsin required notice of claim before § 1983 suit.

name: Printz v. United States
holding: Federal government cannot commandeer state executive officers.
facts_one_liner: Brady Bill required local sheriffs to perform background checks.

name: Alden v. Maine
holding: States retain sovereign immunity from suit in their own courts under congressional commerce power.
facts_one_liner: State probation officers sued state for FLSA overtime.

name: Martin v. Hunter's Lessee
holding: Supreme Court may review state court decisions on federal law.
facts_one_liner: Virginia court refused to obey SC mandate on land grant.

name: Murdock v. Memphis
holding: SC review limited to federal-law questions; state-law remand follows.
facts_one_liner: Land transfer dispute mixed with federal contract claim.

name: Fox Film v. Muller
holding: Adequate and independent state ground bars SC review.
facts_one_liner: State court rested on state contract law and federal antitrust.

name: Michigan v. Long
holding: Presumption of federal-law reliance unless state court explicitly invokes state grounds.
facts_one_liner: Michigan court applied 4A in marijuana possession case without specifying source.

name: Capital Cities Media v. Toole
holding: SC may vacate and remand for clarification of state-law basis.
facts_one_liner: Pennsylvania denied media first-amendment access without clear basis.

name: Indiana ex rel. Anderson v. Brand
holding: SC may review antecedent state-law issues that determine a federal right.
facts_one_liner: Tenured teacher dismissal turned on whether state law created contract.

name: Cardinale v. Louisiana
holding: Federal issue must be raised below for SC review.
facts_one_liner: Murderer first raised constitutional issue before SC.

name: NAACP v. Alabama
holding: State procedural ground inadequate when novel and obstructs federal rights.
facts_one_liner: Alabama held NAACP in contempt for refusing membership lists.

name: Osborn v. Bank of US
holding: Federal ingredient sufficient for Article III FQJ.
facts_one_liner: Ohio auditor sought to enforce state tax on federal bank.

name: Louisville & Nashville v. Mottley
holding: FQJ requires federal question on face of well-pleaded complaint, not in defense.
facts_one_liner: Tort settlement annual passes barred by federal law as defense.

name: American Well Works v. Layne & Bowler
holding: Holmes rule: action arises under law that creates it.
facts_one_liner: State libel claim alleged false statements about federal patent.

name: Smith v. Kansas City Title
holding: Embedded federal question may confer FQJ where federal-law construction is essential.
facts_one_liner: Shareholder suit to enjoin investment in federal bonds.

name: Shoshone Mining v. Rutter
holding: Even where federal law creates claim, embedded state-law issues may defeat FQJ.
facts_one_liner: Mining title dispute on federal land turned on state property law.

name: Merrell Dow v. Thompson
holding: Embedded federal standard insufficient where Congress declined to create PROA.
facts_one_liner: State tort using FDCA mislabeling as negligence per se.

name: Grable v. Darue
holding: Four factors for embedded FQJ: necessarily raised, actually disputed, substantial, balance with Congressional design.
facts_one_liner: Quiet title action turned on adequacy of IRS notice.

name: Gunn v. Minton
holding: Grable factors all required, not balanced; legal-malpractice claim involving patent issue is not substantial.
facts_one_liner: Malpractice in prior patent litigation.

name: National Mutual v. Tidewater Transfer
holding: Congress may grant DC-citizen diversity in federal court (5-4 plurality).
facts_one_liner: DC citizen sued Virginia citizen in federal court.

name: Textile Workers v. Lincoln Mills
holding: § 301 LMRA empowers federal courts to develop labor common law.
facts_one_liner: Suit to enforce collective bargaining no-strike clause.

name: Verlinden v. Central Bank of Nigeria
holding: FSIA grants FQJ over actions against foreign sovereigns.
facts_one_liner: Dutch corporation sued Nigerian central bank.

name: United Mine Workers v. Gibbs
holding: Pendent jurisdiction over state claims sharing common nucleus of operative fact.
facts_one_liner: Labor and state-law tort claims arising from same boycott.

name: Finley v. United States
holding: No pendent-party jurisdiction without express grant.
facts_one_liner: Plane crash plaintiff joined non-federal defendants to FTCA claim.

name: Owen Equipment v. Kroger
holding: Plaintiff may not use ancillary jurisdiction to evade complete-diversity rule.
facts_one_liner: Iowa plaintiff impleaded Iowa defendant in diversity case.

name: Exxon Mobil v. Allapattah
holding: § 1367 allows supplemental jurisdiction over class members below amount-in-controversy.
facts_one_liner: Diversity class action with members below $75k.

name: Swift v. Tyson
holding: Federal courts apply general commercial common law in diversity.
facts_one_liner: Negotiability of bill of exchange in interstate commerce.

name: Erie R.R. v. Tompkins
holding: There is no federal general common law; diversity courts apply state law.
facts_one_liner: Pedestrian struck by train along Pennsylvania track.

name: Gelpcke v. City of Dubuque
holding: Federal court refused to follow state court's overruling of prior decisions.
facts_one_liner: Iowa repudiated bonds previously held valid.

name: Clearfield Trust v. United States
holding: Federal common law governs U.S. commercial paper.
facts_one_liner: Forged government check passed through bank.

name: D'Oench Duhme v. FDIC
holding: Federal common law protects FDIC's interest in bank notes.
facts_one_liner: Side agreement made note unenforceable under state law.

name: United States v. Kimbell Foods
holding: Adopt state law as federal rule when no need for uniformity.
facts_one_liner: Priority of federal SBA lien against state UCC interest.

name: Boyle v. United Technologies
holding: Government contractor defense recognized as federal common law.
facts_one_liner: Marine drowned in defective helicopter escape hatch.

name: Miree v. DeKalb County
holding: State law governs when only incidental U.S. interest in third-party contract.
facts_one_liner: FAA grant required airport restrictions; bird-strike crash.

name: Banco Nacional v. Sabbatino
holding: Act of state doctrine is federal common law.
facts_one_liner: Cuban expropriation of sugar; suit for proceeds.

name: J.I. Case v. Borak
holding: Implied PROA from purposivist reading of '34 Act.
facts_one_liner: Shareholder challenged proxy statement under § 14(a).

name: Cort v. Ash
holding: Four-factor test for implied PROA.
facts_one_liner: Shareholder challenged campaign-finance crime statute.

name: Cannon v. University of Chicago
holding: Title IX includes implied PROA.
facts_one_liner: Female applicant denied medical school admission.

name: Alexander v. Sandoval
holding: Implied PROA depends on text and structure; agency reg cannot create one.
facts_one_liner: Disparate-impact challenge to English-only Alabama drivers tests.

name: Touche Ross v. Redington
holding: Implied PROAs disfavored absent statutory indication.
facts_one_liner: Customers sued auditors under § 17(a) of '34 Act.

name: Bivens v. Six Unknown Named Agents
holding: Damages remedy implied for 4A violation by federal officers.
facts_one_liner: FBI search and arrest without warrant.

name: Davis v. Passman
holding: Bivens action for 5A gender discrimination.
facts_one_liner: Congresswoman fired female staffer for being female.

name: Carlson v. Green
holding: Bivens action for 8A medical-care violations.
facts_one_liner: Federal prisoner died from inadequate care.

name: Bush v. Lucas
holding: No Bivens where civil service remedy exists.
facts_one_liner: NASA engineer demoted for whistleblowing speech.

name: Correctional Services v. Malesko
holding: No Bivens against private corporation operating federal prison.
facts_one_liner: Heart-attack inmate sued private prison company.

name: Ziglar v. Abbasi
holding: Two-step test: new context + special factors counsel hesitation.
facts_one_liner: 9/11 Muslim detainees sued for harsh detention.

name: Hernandez v. Mesa
holding: No Bivens for cross-border shooting; foreign-relations special factor.
facts_one_liner: Border agent shot Mexican boy across border.

name: Egbert v. Boule
holding: Any rational reason for Congressional preference defeats Bivens extension.
facts_one_liner: Border agent assaulted innkeeper near Canadian border.

name: Chisholm v. Georgia
holding: State subject to suit in federal court by out-of-state citizen.
facts_one_liner: South Carolina creditor sued Georgia for debts.

name: Hans v. Louisiana
holding: State immune from suit by its own citizen on federal claim.
facts_one_liner: Louisiana resident sued state for unpaid bond coupons.

name: Ex parte Young
holding: Officer suit fiction permits prospective injunction against unconstitutional state action.
facts_one_liner: Railroad challenged Minnesota rate regulation; AG held in contempt.

name: Edelman v. Jordan
holding: Young allows prospective relief but not retroactive damages from state treasury.
facts_one_liner: Welfare benefits delayed under state procedure.

name: Pennhurst v. Halderman
holding: Young inapplicable to suits seeking to enforce state law against state officer.
facts_one_liner: Class sought conditions improvements under state institutional law.

name: Fitzpatrick v. Bitzer
holding: § 5 of 14A abrogates SSI.
facts_one_liner: Title VII suit against Connecticut.

name: Seminole Tribe v. Florida
holding: Article I Indian Commerce Clause cannot abrogate SSI.
facts_one_liner: Tribe sued Florida under IGRA for refusing good-faith negotiations.

name: Nevada Dept. of HR v. Hibbs
holding: FMLA family-care provision abrogation valid under § 5.
facts_one_liner: State employee denied family medical leave.

name: Tennessee v. Lane
holding: Title II ADA abrogation valid as applied to fundamental access to courts.
facts_one_liner: Paraplegic crawled up stairs to attend court.

name: Central Virginia CC v. Katz
holding: Bankruptcy clause abrogation via plan of convention.
facts_one_liner: Bankruptcy trustee recovered preferential transfers from state college.

name: Torres v. TX DPS
holding: War powers abrogate SSI via plan of convention.
facts_one_liner: USERRA suit by veteran against state employer.

name: PennEast Pipeline v. NJ
holding: Eminent domain power may waive SSI in plan of convention.
facts_one_liner: Pipeline condemnation of state property.

name: Monroe v. Pape
holding: § 1983 reaches state officials acting under color even when violating state law.
facts_one_liner: Chicago police home invasion without warrant.

name: Monell v. NYC Dept Social Services
holding: Municipalities are persons under § 1983 but liable only for policy or custom.
facts_one_liner: NYC required pregnant employees to take unpaid leave.

name: Pembaur v. Cincinnati
holding: Single decision of final policymaker may constitute policy.
facts_one_liner: County prosecutor authorized forced entry into doctor's office.

name: City of Canton v. Harris
holding: Failure-to-train liability requires deliberate indifference.
facts_one_liner: Detainee with medical emergency denied treatment.

name: Connick v. Thompson
holding: Single Brady violation insufficient to show pattern of failure to train.
facts_one_liner: Wrongfully convicted man freed after 18 years on death row.

name: Will v. Michigan State Police
holding: State is not a person under § 1983.
facts_one_liner: State trooper sued state for First Amendment retaliation.

name: Hafer v. Melo
holding: State officer in personal capacity is a person under § 1983.
facts_one_liner: Auditor General fired employees; sued personally.

name: Maine v. Thiboutot
holding: § 1983 reaches violations of federal statutes generally.
facts_one_liner: Welfare recipients sued state for Social Security Act violation.

name: Middlesex County Sewerage v. NSA
holding: Comprehensive remedial scheme bars supplemental § 1983 enforcement.
facts_one_liner: Sea clammers sued under § 1983 for FWPCA pollution.

name: Gonzaga v. Doe
holding: Federal statute creates § 1983-enforceable right only with rights-creating language.
facts_one_liner: Student sued under FERPA after counselor disclosed information.

name: Health & Hospital Corp. v. Talevski
holding: Spending Clause statute can be § 1983-enforceable when rights-creating.
facts_one_liner: Nursing home patient family sued under FNHRA.

name: Blessing v. Freestone
holding: Three-factor test for § 1983 federal-statute claims.
facts_one_liner: Mothers sued state child support agency.

name: Harlow v. Fitzgerald
holding: Modern QI: clearly established law a reasonable officer would know.
facts_one_liner: White House aides sued for retaliation against whistleblower.

name: Nixon v. Fitzgerald
holding: President has absolute civil immunity for official acts.
facts_one_liner: Whistleblower sued Nixon for reorganization firing.

name: Trump v. United States
holding: President has criminal immunity for core official acts; presumptive for outer acts.
facts_one_liner: January 6 indictments.

name: Clinton v. Jones
holding: No civil immunity for unofficial acts.
facts_one_liner: Civil suit for pre-presidency conduct.

name: Pierson v. Ray
holding: Judges absolutely immune from § 1983 damages.
facts_one_liner: Civil rights plaintiffs sued judge for jailing them.

name: Stump v. Sparkman
holding: Judicial immunity covers even outrageous orders within jurisdiction.
facts_one_liner: Judge ordered sterilization of teenager without notice.

name: Imbler v. Pachtman
holding: Prosecutors absolutely immune for advocacy functions.
facts_one_liner: Wrongfully convicted man sued for using perjured testimony.

name: Buckley v. Fitzsimmons
holding: Investigative tasks not protected by absolute immunity.
facts_one_liner: Prosecutor fabricated evidence pre-charge.

name: Mitchell v. Forsyth
holding: Denial of QI immediately appealable.
facts_one_liner: AG ordered warrantless wiretap.

name: Anderson v. Creighton
holding: Clearly established law must be sufficiently particularized to facts.
facts_one_liner: FBI agent sued for warrantless search.

name: Saucier v. Katz
holding: Mandatory two-step QI sequence (overruled by Pearson).
facts_one_liner: Officer pushed protester near VP.

name: Pearson v. Callahan
holding: Courts may decide QI prongs in any order.
facts_one_liner: Officer entered apartment with informant consent.

name: Taylor v. Riojas
holding: Egregious conditions are obviously unconstitutional even without on-point case.
facts_one_liner: Inmate held in feces-covered cell.

name: Hope v. Pelzer
holding: Egregious conduct does not require on-point case for clearly established.
facts_one_liner: Inmate handcuffed to hitching post for hours.

name: Kisela v. Hughes
holding: QI protects split-second use-of-force decisions absent specific precedent.
facts_one_liner: Officer shot woman with knife in yard.

name: Brown v. Allen
holding: Federal habeas reaches all federal questions, including factual ones.
facts_one_liner: Three death row petitioners sought review.

name: Stone v. Powell
holding: 4A claims not cognizable on habeas if state provided full and fair litigation.
facts_one_liner: State prisoners challenged Fourth Amendment rulings.

name: Wainwright v. Sykes
holding: Cause and prejudice standard for procedural default.
facts_one_liner: Failure to object to confession admission.

name: Coleman v. Thompson
holding: Attorney error in collateral proceedings not cause for default.
facts_one_liner: Late filing in state collateral review.

name: Murray v. Carrier
holding: Attorney error short of IATC not cause for procedural default.
facts_one_liner: Counsel omitted issue on appeal.

name: McQuiggin v. Perkins
holding: Actual innocence excuses AEDPA's one-year limitations.
facts_one_liner: Petitioner produced new affidavits.

name: Martinez v. Ryan
holding: Ineffective assistance at initial-review collateral may be cause.
facts_one_liner: Arizona's bar on direct-appeal IATC raised on collateral.

name: Shinn v. Ramirez
holding: § 2254(e)(2) limits new-evidence development for IATC excused under Martinez.
facts_one_liner: Innocence claims with new evidence in Arizona cases.

name: Teague v. Lane
holding: New rules of constitutional criminal procedure don't apply retroactively on habeas.
facts_one_liner: Jury composition challenge.

name: Felker v. Turpin
holding: AEDPA's bar on second petitions does not violate Suspension Clause.
facts_one_liner: Death-row petitioner barred from second petition.

name: Williams v. Taylor
holding: § 2254(d)(1): contrary to vs. unreasonable application standards.
facts_one_liner: Death-row IATC claim.

name: Harrington v. Richter
holding: Even summary state-court denial gets AEDPA deference.
facts_one_liner: Murder conviction with summary state denial.

name: Cullen v. Pinholster
holding: § 2254(d)(1) review limited to record before state court.
facts_one_liner: Death-row petitioner sought new evidence in federal court.

name: Hamdi v. Rumsfeld
holding: U.S. citizen detainee entitled to due process to challenge enemy-combatant designation.
facts_one_liner: American-born Saudi held at Guantanamo.

name: Rasul v. Bush
holding: § 2241 habeas reaches Guantanamo.
facts_one_liner: British and Australian Guantanamo detainees.

name: Fay v. Noia
holding: Pre-Wainwright deliberate-bypass standard for default.
facts_one_liner: Murder conviction confession.
